articles.summary: The game of basketball can be complex. But often, its solutions can be simple. articles.summary: The Mavericks are hiring former Blazers assistant GM Mike Schmitz to run the franchise's basketball operations under president Masai Ujiri.

articles.title: NBA hopeful Peterson: Creatine led to cramping
articles.original_title: NBA hopeful Peterson: Creatine led to cramping
articles.summary: Darryn Peterson said a new round of bloodwork and other tests after the season led his doctors to conclude that his use of high doses of creatine led to mysterious and sometimes debilitating cramping during his freshman season at Kansas.

articles.title: Sixers donate playoff tix to evade Knicks fans
articles.original_title: Sixers donate playoff tix to evade Knicks fans
articles.summary: The 76ers will donate 500 tickets for each remaining home second-round playoff game to area community groups in their latest attempt to keep more of their own fans in the building and avoid a takeover by Knicks supporters.

articles.title: Thunder Lead Series 2-0 as Holmgren, SGA Score 22
articles.original_title: OKC up 2-0 on Lakers as Holmgren, SGA score 22
articles.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder seized control in Game 2 against the Los Angeles Lakers, moving the series to 2-0. Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander sat out much of the fourth quarter due to foul trouble, while Chet Holmgren and SGA each contributed 22 points.
key_points: Thunder outscored Lakers and took a 2‑0 series lead.
Chet Holmgren and Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander each finished with 22 points.
Gilgeous‑Alexander was limited by foul trouble in the final minutes.

articles.title: Redick and Reaves Criticize Referees After Lakers’ Loss
articles.original_title: Lakers' Redick rips refs; Reaves takes issue, too
articles.summary: Head coach JJ Redick publicly condemned the officiating following the Lakers’ Game 2 defeat to the Thunder, and guard Austin Reaves echoed his frustration. Both cited specific calls they felt disadvantaged Los Angeles.
key_points: Redick called the refereeing "inconsistent" and "impactful" on the outcome.
Reaves added that several calls hurt the Lakers’ chances in the fourth quarter.
The comments came after a loss that put the Lakers down 0‑2 in the series.

articles.title: Cade Cunningham Leads Pistons Past Cavaliers in Game 2
articles.original_title: Cunningham, Pistons pull away, take G2 vs. Cavs
articles.summary: Cade Cunningham scored heavily as the Detroit Pistons defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers to take a 2‑0 series edge. The Pistons, who were near the bottom of the standings a week earlier, continued their surge.
key_points: Cunningham posted a strong scoring night, helping Detroit pull away late.
The win gave the Pistons a 2‑0 lead over the Cavaliers.
Detroit’s recent turnaround follows a period where their playoff hopes were in doubt.

articles.title: Harden Struggles in Pistons Loss as Cavs Seek Answers
articles.original_title: Cavs plan to 'figure it out' after Harden struggle...
articles.summary: James Harden continued his postseason slump, going 3‑for‑13 from the field and missing all four three‑point attempts in the Cavaliers’ 107‑97 defeat to Detroit. The poor performance added pressure on Cleveland to adjust.
key_points: Harden shot 23% (3‑of‑13) and was 0‑for‑4 from three.
Cavaliers fell 10 points to the Pistons, dropping the series to 0‑2.
The loss highlighted ongoing offensive issues for Cleveland.

articles.title: Offseason Buzz: Durant’s Ankle and Other NBA Updates
articles.original_title: 🐝 Latest buzz ahead of the offseason, draft
articles.summary: A roundup of the latest NBA offseason rumors and insider reactions, featuring an update on Kevin Durant’s lingering ankle injury. The briefing also covers draft projections and other player news.
key_points: Kevin Durant’s ankle injury remains a concern ahead of free agency.
Insiders discuss potential draft moves and team strategies.
Additional updates include trade rumors and contract negotiations across the league.
